stories.title: A Legendary Grand Canyon Explorer Vanished in 2012. Authorities Just Found His Remains.
stories.summary: Human remains found on the Hualapai Indian Reservation in December 2025 have been confirmed as those of 65-year-old Oregon hiker Bill Ott, who vanished during a solo Grand Canyon trek in April 2012. Ott, a legendary wilderness figure and experienced hiker, had spent years exploring the Grand Canyon and documenting ancient rock art. His remains were discovered near a trail at the base of a 15-foot cliff, with a small daypack containing his wallet, cash, camera, and GPS. The cause of death remains unknown.

stories.title: McDonald’s new Caesar menu arrives in NY. See what’s included
stories.summary: McDonald's has launched its new Caesar menu nationwide, including in New York, on Tuesday, July 21. The menu features a new Caesar sauce, a creamy garlic and parmesan blend, and includes items such as the Caesar Snack Wrap, a Bacon Caesar McCrispy Sandwich, and crispy McCrispy Strips. The new menu items will be available for a limited time at participating McDonald's restaurants. Customers can try the new menu for $2.99, starting today.

stories.title: ‘Disney Worldbuilders’ Documentary by Leslie Iwerks, Featuring James Cameron, Kevin Feige and Jon Favreau, Set to Premiere on Disney+
stories.summary: A new documentary "Disney Worldbuilders" by Leslie Iwerks is set to premiere on Disney+ on August 20. The film takes viewers behind the scenes of how Disney movies and franchises like "Frozen" and "Zootopia" are transformed into immersive experiences at the Disney parks. Featuring insights from James Cameron, Kevin Feige, and Jon Favreau, the documentary showcases the creative process behind Disney's theme park attractions. The result is a unique look at the intersection of film and fantasy.

stories.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
stories.summary: Apple's high-end 13-inch iPad Pro M5 with 512GB of storage is currently discounted by $150 at Amazon and $100 at Best Buy, now priced at $1,549.99. This premium iPad features an M5 processor, OLED screen, and support for the Apple Pencil Pro and Magic Keyboard. The 11-inch iPad Air M3 with 1TB of storage is also on sale, marked down by $500 to $749.

stories.title: Hollywood’s Favorite Ugg Slippers (To Never Wear on a Christopher Nolan Set)
stories.summary: Christopher Nolan has banned Ugg slippers from his film sets, citing they can "weirdly take you out of reality." This policy was evident in his recent film "The Odyssey," where actors wore sandals instead of Uggs to maintain historical accuracy. The ban applies to all sets, including the upcoming film. The decision aims to keep actors immersed in their characters' ancient Greek world.

stories.title: Burnham stirs up political row with review of release plans for violent criminals
stories.summary: British Prime Minister Andy Burnham has ordered a review of plans to release thousands of violent criminals from prison early, affecting around 6,000 offenders. The move follows outrage from the family of PC Andrew Harper, whose killers may be eligible for release. The review aims to address concerns over public safety and the potential collapse of the criminal justice system. The outcome of the review is expected to be announced in the coming months.

stories.title: Wood Brothers Racing – Race Report: North Wilkesboro Speedway
stories.summary: At North Wilkesboro Speedway in North Carolina, Josh Berry finished 36th in the Window World 450 on Sunday, July 19, 2026, after a promising start that saw him climb from 28th to 12th before a caution flag trapped him multiple laps down. Berry's team had made a clean green-flag pit stop, but the timing proved costly. The result marked another stroke of misfortune for the No. 21 Motorcraft/Quick Lane Ford Mustang Dark Horse.

stories.title: Baltimore Orioles at Boston Red Sox Game Story, Scores/Highlights - 07/20/2026
stories.summary: The Baltimore Orioles took the lead in the top of the 5th inning with Christian Encarnacion-Strand's first home run as an Oriole, giving the Orioles a 2-3 advantage over the Boston Red Sox. The Orioles' Tyler O'Neill had an RBI ground-rule double in the top of the 1st inning. The Red Sox responded with a 3-run 3rd inning, capped by Willson Contreras' RBI single. The Orioles look to build on their momentum in the second half of the game.

stories.title: iOS code could reportedly let Apple cut off apps when users miss iPhone payments
stories.summary: Apple is reportedly planning to introduce a new financing program for leasing iPhones, with a feature that would allow the company to restrict access to nearly all apps on a device if payments are missed. The "Restricted Mode" would be triggered by a new "App Managed Features" system that continuously checks the device's payment status. This feature is part of iOS 27, currently in beta, and would affect users who participate in the "Apple Upgrade" program. If implemented, users would lose access to most apps, except for basic functions.

stories.title: Bose Wants to Be the Centerpiece of Your Living Room Again With the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker
stories.summary: Bose has launched the Lifestyle Ultra Speaker, a flagship wireless speaker now available at Best Buy for $299. The speaker is part of the reimagined Lifestyle Collection and is currently a bestseller at the retailer. It can be expanded into a multi-room setup or full home theater over time. The company's proprietary CleanBass technology delivers fuller low-end performance in the speaker.

stories.title: Teenager drops social media addiction lawsuit against Meta
stories.summary: A Florida teenager, 15-year-old R.K.C., has dropped his lawsuit against Meta, citing concerns about a lengthy jury trial, after previously settling similar claims against TikTok, Snapchat, and YouTube. The case, which aimed to hold social media companies accountable for addictive platforms harming children, will no longer proceed. R.K.C.'s lawyers say he is now focusing on his recovery and therapy. A Meta spokesperson claims the lawsuit was baseless.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2023 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend in the Dordogne village where she lived. Prosecutors say the woman was originally taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. The case will now proceed to trial if the investigating judge finds sufficient evidence.

stories.title: Villa agree loan deal for Chelsea winger Garnacho
stories.summary: Aston Villa has agreed a loan deal for Chelsea winger Alejandro Garnacho until the end of the 2026-27 season, with an obligation to buy for £43m. The 22-year-old has completed a medical in Birmingham and pre-agreed a four-year contract at Villa Park. Garnacho's departure from Chelsea follows the club's signing of Morgan Rogers for a record £117m fee from Villa. The winger becomes Villa's fourth signing this summer, following a series of player sales that have totalled nearly £200m.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on ships in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The potential targets include those located near the Iranian capital Tehran. This warning comes after the US launched strikes on Iran for the 11th consecutive night. Iran's air defences were activated in Tehran following the US strikes.

stories.title: Tories ask HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage owes tax on £5m gift
stories.summary: Tories have asked HMRC to investigate whether Nigel Farage should have paid tax on a £5m gift from crypto billionaire Christopher Harborne. The gift was reportedly given with no expectation of return, but Farage has previously stated he would require £1m per year in compensation to offset loss of earnings if he ran for office. HMRC will now review the matter. The outcome of this investigation remains to be seen.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA is set to unveil its first new wind tunnel in over 40 years at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The Flight Dynamics Research Facility will be showcased in a media tour and ribbon-cutting ceremony. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and center director Dr. Trina Dyal will be available for media questions. The event marks a significant milestone in NASA's evolution.

stories.title: Rangers to fight McInnes ban for 'disgusting' penalty remark
stories.summary: Rangers manager Derek McInnes has been given a four-match touchline ban by the Scottish FA for calling a late penalty decision "disgusting" in May. The comments were made after Celtic were awarded a stoppage-time spot-kick in a 3-2 victory at Motherwell, which kept them one point behind Hearts. McInnes is due to serve the ban, with one game suspended until the end of the season. He will be in the stands as Rangers open the Scottish Premiership campaign.

stories.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris
stories.summary: A modelling scout linked to Jeffrey Epstein, Daniel Siad, 69, has been found dead at his home in Colombes, a north-west suburb of Paris. An investigation into his death was opened on Monday evening, with a post-mortem examination to be carried out. Siad was previously investigated by the Paris prosecutor's office for organised human trafficking and conspiracy charges, but was not arrested due to lack of evidence. The investigation into his death will likely shed light on the circumstances surrounding his death.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Inter Miami is under investigation by Major League Soccer for alleged tampering after signing Brazil midfielder Casemiro. The investigation concerns the club's acquisition of Casemiro's discovery rights from the LA Galaxy. Casemiro signed a deal with Inter Miami that runs until the end of the 2027 MLS campaign. The league will review all relevant information before making a decision.

stories.title: Dubai Firm Cuts Deal for New Port to Bypass Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: A Dubai-based logistics giant unveiled a deal with the United Arab Emirates on Wednesday to develop a new port system that would bypass the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Laredo’s Future Rides on Trump’s Next Trade Move
stories.summary: The economy of Laredo, Texas, has boomed under the president’s global tariffs. Now, his demands may threaten its success.

stories.title: Israeli Banks Threaten to Cut Off Palestinian Economy
stories.summary: Two Israeli financial firms have long served as partners to Palestinian banks, handling transactions that make possible the West Bank’s imports and exports.

stories.title: Hitler’s Birthplace in Austria Is Now a Police Station
stories.summary: The government repurposed the 17th century building in hopes of preventing the site from becoming an attraction for Nazi sympathizers.

stories.title: Sony Revives Cinerama Dome in Los Angeles as Box Office Sales Rebound
stories.summary: The iconic Cinerama Dome and adjacent 14-screen theater complex will reopen in 2028 after a renovation. The venue closed in 2020 because of the pandemic.

stories.title: Trump’s Trade Negotiator Doubles Down on Tariffs Before Global Duties Expire
stories.summary: Jamieson Greer, the president’s top trade negotiator, told the Senate that the United States remained intent on tariffs as it prepared to replace duties the Supreme Court struck down.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report finds that obesity is an engineered outcome, not a result of individual willpower. The report highlights that ultra-processed foods, particularly those high in sugar and refined carbohydrates, are primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The committee recommends targeting these foods to reduce obesity in Britain. The government is expected to implement new policies to hold supermarkets and manufacturers accountable for healthier food options.

stories.title: Fifth person dies in New York City amid legionnaires’ disease outbreak
stories.summary: A fifth person has died in New York City's legionnaires' disease outbreak, with 82 cases now identified on Manhattan's Upper East Side. The outbreak is linked to 34 cooling towers across 33 buildings, where live legionella bacteria were detected. Eight people remain hospitalized, while 56 have been discharged. The city's health department will continue testing the remaining 140 cooling tower systems.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
